DECLARATION OF CHERYL CARLSON IN SUPPORT OF PETITIONER MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 100119181) FILED BY PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS ASSOCIATION ON BEHALF OF ITSELF AND ITS MEMBERS

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED. THE CITY SHALL PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE, NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS, INCLUDING ESI DOCUMENTS BY FEBRUARY 5, 2021. ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD ON PRIVILEGE SHALL BE PLACED ON A LOG. PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500. COUNSEL FOR THE PETITIONER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG PRIOR TO THE HEARING EVEN IF THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED.

Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. The City shall produce all responsive, non-privileged documents, including ESI documents by February 5, 2021. Any documents withheld on privilege shall be placed on a log. Petitioner's motion for sanctions is granted in the amount of $2,500.

Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: proceedings not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-20-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jeffrey R.A. Edwards of the law offices of Mastagni Holstedt (916.491.4217) appears as counsel for petitioner/moving party, San Francisco Sheriffs Association

Attorney, Rafal Ofierski of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415.554.4244) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party, City and County of San Francisco

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS ASSOCIATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION.

*********

Later this sam date and after calendar call:

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association's motion for preliminary injunction is DENIED. In determining whether to issue the injunction, the court considers the likelihood that Association will prevail on the merits and the balance of equities and harms. (See Metro Traffic Control, Inc. v. Shadow Traffic Network (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 853, 858.) In this case, the court finds that Association is unlikely to prevail on the merits and show that defendants' paired gender identity search policy violates the law.
The court rejects Association's privacy argument. "[T]he Constitution does indeed protect the right to maintain the confidentiality of one's transsexualism." (Powell v Schriver (2d. Cir. 1999) 175 F.3d 107, 111.) Here, the undisputed evidence shows that deputies are not required to disclose anything and they are assigned to conduct searches by how they identify and present themselves at work, which is the same way the former search policy worked. (Freeman Dec. pars. 5-7.) The paired gender identity search policy does not violate Penal Code sec. 4030(k). First, Association lacks standing to raise the issue because there is no realistic danger that Association or its members will sustain direct injury; i.e., there is no credible threat that Penal Code sec. 4030(m) will be invoked against Association and its members. (See Prigmore v. City of Redding (2012) 211 Cal.App.4th 1322, 1349.) (CCSF's Request for Judicial Notice Exs. A and B, Opinions of DA and AG.) Second, "[o]pinions of the Attorney General, while not binding, are entitled to great weight. In the absence of controlling authority, these opinions are persuasive 'since the Legislature is presumed to be cognizant of that construction of the statute'" (California Assn. of Psychology Providers v. Rank (1990) 51 Cal.3d 1, 17.) Here, the opinions of the DA and AG that "sex" as used in Penal Code sec. 4030 includes gender identity is reasonable, correct in light of Penal Code sec. 422.56(c), and the court adopts that interpretation. Therefore, defendants' policy does not violate the code. The proposed Written order heretofor submitted by the prevailing party is signed and filed this date. Email notice by clerk. (**ruling not reported**)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Deborah Neville, CSR No. 9703, e-mail: dneville@sftc.org (L&M302)
